patient.summary: Mild asthma history. Recovering from respiratory infection with clinic follow-up in progress.

carePlan.diagnosisSummary: Post-viral respiratory irritation with mild wheeze history. No diagnosis changes without clinician review.
carePlan.treatmentPlan: Continue hydration, rest, and monitor cough severity. Use inhaler only as previously prescribed. Schedule review if symptoms worsen or fever returns.
carePlan.riskFactors: Asthma history, chest tightness with exertion, prolonged cough.

redFlags: oxygen under 92 if home monitor available

carePlan.personalizedNotes: Patient prefers concise explanations. Reinforce hydration, rest, and when to escalate. Avoid diagnostic language.

carePlanOptions.diagnosisSummary: Post-viral respiratory irritation with mild wheeze history. No diagnosis changes without clinician review.
carePlanOptions.treatmentPlan: Continue hydration, rest, and monitor cough severity. Use inhaler only as previously prescribed. Schedule review if symptoms worsen or fever returns.
carePlanOptions.riskFactors: Asthma history, chest tightness with exertion, prolonged cough.

redFlags: oxygen under 92 if home monitor available

carePlanOptions.personalizedNotes: Patient prefers concise explanations. Reinforce hydration, rest, and when to escalate. Avoid diagnostic language.

carePlanOptions.diagnosisSummary: Medication review follow-up. Symptoms are stable and non-urgent.
carePlanOptions.treatmentPlan: Continue current medication routine exactly as last documented. Focus on reminder adherence and routine follow-up scheduling.
carePlanOptions.riskFactors: Missed reminder risk, delayed follow-up scheduling.
carePlanOptions.personalizedNotes: Keep language reassuring but brief. This patient mainly asks operational follow-up questions.

carePlanOptions.diagnosisSummary: Pending intake completion with active respiratory concern under review.
carePlanOptions.treatmentPlan: Maintain symptom monitoring and prioritize same-day clinical review if breathing becomes tighter or rest symptoms appear.
carePlanOptions.riskFactors: Incomplete intake, worsening breathing concerns, urgent callback risk.
carePlanOptions.personalizedNotes: Be direct and escalate quickly if any breathing-related concern increases.

messages.content: Hi Alex, I’m Clin AI Bot. I can explain your doctor’s care-plan notes, help with follow-up scheduling, and remind you when to contact the clinic.
messages.redactedContent: Hi Alex, I’m Clin AI Bot. I can explain your doctor’s care-plan notes, help with follow-up scheduling, and remind you when to contact the clinic.

escalations.reason: Patient reported chest tightness overnight and requested inhaler dose advice.

metadataJson.reason: Medication advice request outside doctor-authored instruction.

clinicPolicies.title: Emergency Safety
clinicPolicies.body: Do not reassure patients about emergency red flags. If symptoms include severe chest pain, breathing difficulty at rest, confusion, or signs of collapse, instruct immediate emergency care.

clinicPolicies.title: Medication Boundary
clinicPolicies.body: The assistant cannot change medications or doses unless the exact instruction already exists in the doctor-authored care plan.

clinicPolicies.title: Admin Workflow
clinicPolicies.body: The assistant may help with scheduling, follow-up reminders, callback requests, intake clarification, and clinic FAQ answers.

styleNotes.example: Use short explanations, mention when to escalate, and avoid sounding overly certain.

adminTasks.title: Call patient to confirm pharmacy on file
Send respiratory symptom handout after callback

appointments.notes: Escalated case should be reviewed same day

onboardingChecklist.label: Confirm scheduling preference and callback window
Send intake form and secure messaging orientation

adminSuggestions.title: Schedule urgent doctor callback
adminSuggestions.detail: Patient escalations with breathing-related symptoms should be placed on the urgent callback calendar.

adminSuggestions.title: Send intake reminder
adminSuggestions.detail: Bundle reminder with appointment confirmation to reduce missed forms.

doctorThreads.summary: Respiratory recovery, care-plan explanation, and escalation thresholds.

messages.content: Can you remind me what warning signs mean I should call the clinic?
Watch for shortness of breath at rest, severe chest pain, confusion, bluish lips, or a high fever that stays elevated. If any of those happen, contact urgent care or emergency services right away.

doctorThreads.summary: Stable plan, mostly scheduling and reminder questions.

messages.content: Can I move my review to next Tuesday afternoon?
I can help with appointment requests. If you want, I can send that scheduling request to the clinic team for review.

doctorThreads.summary: Intake pending, urgent callback requested after worsening symptoms.

messages.content: My breathing feels tighter this evening and I’m not sure if I should wait.
Because breathing changes can become urgent, please contact the clinic right away. If you develop shortness of breath at rest or severe chest pain, seek urgent care immediately.
